126 Brhat Samhitll

There are 25 comets named Kiranas, born of the Sun,
resembling a pearl-necklace, gems or gold. They are possessed of
crests and appear in the east and .west. They are disastrous to the
rulers of the land.
[Cf. ^r—
^T^Tg^nrrfvrr: ^fwr: R^rfWct: U
f^rrr^rr 5^1 snfeftr ftwr: 1
^rr -^im^ 11
The commentator adds here a note to the effect that only
one of the 25 comets appears at a time, and not all at once. This
rule applies to all groups of comets.]
grTRRfrr: i
m*knn cTT^T^sfq ftrfe^T^r: U??M
There are 25 other comets born of the fire and resembling
the parrot (blue and yellow), the fire, the Bandhujiva flower (which
is deep red), lac and blood in colour. They make their appear-
ance in the South-east and portend fire-disasters.
[These comets are the children of the fire whose direction
is the South-east.
